title: Writing - Part 2
description: Choose one of the following writing tasks and write your answer in 140-190 words.

tasks.title: Letter
tasks.prompt:
You recently attended a hobby fair at a local community centre, but found it poorly organised and disappointing. Write a letter to the community centre manager explaining what went wrong, the impact this had on your experience, and suggesting how future events could be better organised.

Write your letter.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Dear Manager,

I am writing regarding the Hobby and Crafts Fair held at the Riverside Community Centre last Saturday. I attended with high expectations, as I had seen the event advertised widely in the local area. Unfortunately, my experience was extremely disappointing, and I felt I should bring my concerns to your attention.

The most significant problem was the lack of clear signage. On arriving, I had no idea where different stalls were located, and neither did several other visitors I spoke with. Additionally, many of the activities listed on the programme were not actually running on the day, which was frustrating for those who had come specifically to try them.

The queuing system was also poorly managed, resulting in long waits and a generally chaotic atmosphere that made it difficult to enjoy the event.

For future events, I would suggest providing a clear map at the entrance, confirming all activities beforehand, and training staff to direct visitors efficiently. With these improvements, the fair could be genuinely excellent.

Yours sincerely,
Margaret Lawson

tasks.title: Article
tasks.prompt:
You see this notice in an English-language magazine:

'Hobbies can transform your life! Write an article about a hobby you are passionate about, explaining how you got started, what you enjoy most about it, and why you would encourage others to try it.'

Write your article.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Why I Can't Stop Baking — And Why You Shouldn't Either

It started, as many good things do, during a rainy afternoon with nothing to do and a bag of flour on the shelf. Three years later, baking has become one of the most important parts of my life — a creative outlet, a stress reliever, and a source of enormous satisfaction.

What I love most about baking is the combination of science and art. Every recipe is a small experiment, and every finished loaf or cake is something tangible you have created with your own hands. In a world where most of our work happens on screens and disappears the moment we close a tab, there is something deeply satisfying about producing something real and delicious.

Baking also has a wonderful social dimension. Sharing what you have made with friends, family, or colleagues creates instant happiness. I have never given someone homemade bread and received anything other than a huge smile.

If you have never tried baking, start small — a simple banana bread or a batch of shortbread. I promise you will be hooked within a week.

tasks.title: Review
tasks.prompt:
You see this notice on a website:

'We are looking for reviews of places where people can pursue hobbies — such as art classes, music venues, sports clubs, or craft workshops. Write a review of a place or class you have attended and say whether you would recommend it.'

Write your review.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Review: The Clay Studio — Pottery Classes for All Levels

I discovered The Clay Studio six months ago when searching for a creative hobby that would help me switch off from work. Since then, I have attended fortnightly wheel-throwing classes and can honestly say it has been one of the best decisions I have made.

The studio is a warm, welcoming space with excellent facilities. The potter's wheels are well-maintained, and all materials are included in the class price. The instructor, Miriam, is exceptionally knowledgeable and patient, offering individual feedback to each student regardless of their experience level. The small class sizes — a maximum of eight people — mean you genuinely receive personalised attention.

The community atmosphere is another major highlight. I have met a wonderfully diverse group of people, and the shared concentration required for pottery creates an unusually peaceful, almost meditative environment.

At £25 per two-hour session, the classes are reasonable value given the materials and expertise included. Beginners are very welcome — no previous experience is needed.

Highly recommended for anyone seeking a creative, calming hobby. Rating: 5/5
